source: goarmy.com, army.mil, specialforces.americanspecialops.com, operationmilitarykids.org 2026, shadowspear.com, dfas.mil

stages.title: Infantry One Station Unit Training (OSUT)

phases.content: Physical conditioning, ACFT baseline, weapons familiarization (M4, M249, M240B), land navigation basics, first aid, Army values, discipline, barracks inspections
Infantry-specific: small unit tactics, squad/platoon live fire, anti-armor (Javelin), night operations, field training exercises, M4 expert qualification required

stages.note_11X_vs_11B: 11X is the entry code. During OSUT, Army classifies: ~85% become 11B (Infantryman), ~15% become 11C (Indirect Fire Infantryman — 81mm mortar). Soldier has limited say.
stages.note_11C_future: Recruiter reports suggest 11C may eventually be phased into vehicle-mounted systems. Currently still active MOS.

stages.title: Basic Airborne Course (BAC) — Jump School

phases.name: Ground Week
phases.content: PLF (Parachute Landing Fall) drills, mock door, 34-ft tower, physical fitness test (APFT: 60 pts per event age 17-21 standard)

phases.name: Tower Week
phases.content: Swing Landing Trainer (SLT) qualification, 250-ft free tower, mass exit from 34-ft tower, canopy manipulation

phases.name: Jump Week
phases.content: 5 qualifying static-line jumps from C-130/C-17 at 1,250 ft AGL. Includes one night jump. Earn airborne wings on 5th jump.

stages.pay_add: Jump pay: $150/mo while assigned to airborne billet or in jump status

stages.title: Special Forces Preparation Course (SFPC)
stages.purpose: Physical and mental ramp-up to SFAS standards. Failing to meet standards by end of SFPC = removed from 18X pipeline.

stages.title: Special Forces Assessment and Selection (SFAS)
stages.rank_eligible: E-2 to E-7 (18X enter as E-2/E-3; in-service applicants E-4 to E-7)
stages.pass_rate: ~20-30% of all entrants receive Q Course seats
stages.2026_changes: Extended from 24 to 35 days. Team Week now 8 days. Land nav +72-96 hrs with 3 STAR attempts. Unknown run/ruck events up to 40% longer. Weekly peer evaluations — lowest-rated candidate cut weekly.
key_events: Gate Week — graded unknown ruck marches with increasing weight
Land Navigation — solo map and compass only, cross-country, 72-96 hrs total
Team Week (8 days) — SF mission task sets in small teams
Swim assessment: 50m in boots and BDU + 11-min water tread + 4-min flotation
administrative_requirements: E-4 to E-7 for in-service; 18X contract for direct-entry

stages.title: Special Forces Qualification Course (Q Course) — Full Pipeline
stages.note: Duration depends heavily on MOS track (18D is longest at 50+ wks for MOS phase alone) and language training length

phases.content: Tactical Skills, small unit tactics, SF Tactics, SERE intro, language/culture intro, in-process at Fort Liberty

18B_weapons_sgt.content: All infantry/crew-served/foreign weapons, anti-armor, improvised weapons

18C_engineer_sgt.content: Demolitions, construction, breaching, IED defeat, bridge building

18D_medical_sgt.content: Physician assistant-level medical training. SOCM. Combat trauma, surgery, dental, vet. Most respected/longest MOS in SF.

18E_comms_sgt.content: SATCOM, HF, VHF, crypto, PACE planning. Best civilian bridge of any SF MOS.

18F_intel_sgt.note: Available only after holding another 18-series MOS

phases.content: Assigned by DLAB score and SF Group regional alignment. Higher DLAB opens more options.
Unconventional Warfare culmination exercise. Real civilians as role players. Multiple NC counties. Realistic behind-lines ODA mission. Pass = Green Beret.

stages.title: Ranger Assessment and Selection Program 1 (RASP 1)

phases.content: Physical and psychological evaluation, road marches, land nav, combatives, medical first responder, Ranger history. Key events: 12-mile ruck march w/35lb pack, 15-meter swim in full uniform.
Ranger skills: direct action, small boat ops, special operations tasks, crew-served weapons, Ranger-specific TTPs. Key events: 5-mile run in 40 min, 12-mile road march.

stages.note: RASP 1 = enlisted E1-E5. RASP 2 = NCOs E6+ and officers.

stages.title: US Army Ranger School

phases.name: Benning Phase
phases.content: RAP week (physical screening: push-ups, sit-ups, pull-ups, 5-mile run, water survival, combat water survival). Patrol planning and execution, raids, ambushes, squad tactics.

phases.name: Mountain Phase
phases.location: Camp Frank D. Merrill, Dahlonega GA
phases.content: Mountain patrolling, rappelling, mountaineering, cold weather survival, leadership under sustained sleep/food deprivation.

phases.name: Swamp Phase
phases.content: Jungle/swamp patrolling, boat operations, water crossings, final graded leadership positions.

stages.note: Ranger School = Ranger TAB (leadership school, open to any MOS). Ranger Regiment = Ranger SCROLL (unit, requires RASP). Different credentials. Most Regiment Rangers eventually attend Ranger School and earn both.

stages.title: US Army Sniper School
stages.location: Fort Moore GA — Harmony Church area
stages.content: Marksmanship training 300m to 1500m, stalking and ghillie concealment, range estimation, sniper team planning and employment, observation and surveillance, ballistics data books.
weapons_used: M2010 Enhanced Sniper Rifle (.300 Win Mag)

stages.title: Delta Force / 1st SFOD-D Selection + OTC
requirements: Pass 5-event PFT in BDUs: run-dodge-jump, inverted crawl, push-ups, sit-ups, 2-mile run
selection_events: Progressively longer/heavier solo ruck marches in Appalachian Mountains
No announced time limits or distances
The Long Walk — final event: ~40-mile ruck with 45-lb pack, mountainous terrain
stages.otc_content: 6-month Operator Training Course. Advanced CQB (close quarters battle), vehicle assault, HALO, hostage rescue, advanced demolitions, foreign language, targeting, intelligence collection.
stages.note: Failure in OTC (non-integrity) still allows candidates to attend Q Course for 18-series MOS.
